title: Cursor Dynamic Workflows
description: Use Cursor as an MCP host for you-agent-factory Factory Session and dynamic-workflow tools.
openingSummary: Connect Cursor to you-agent-factory over Model Context Protocol (MCP) so you can drive Factory Session and dynamic-workflow tools from the editor.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Cursor dynamic workflows mean using Cursor as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) host for you-agent-factory. Cursor launches you mcp serve as a child process, then calls Factory Session tools that validate, start, and inspect JavaScript or TypeScript dynamic workflows. In this product, a dynamic workflow is a JavaScript orchestrator-backed factory; the live run is still a Factory Session. The MCP path keeps that work inside the editor instead of switching to a separate terminal for every validate, start, or status step.

whenToUse.title: When To Use
whenToUse.body: Use this path when you author or drive JavaScript or TypeScript dynamic workflows from Cursor and want Factory Session tools available as MCP tools in the editor. Prefer ordinary factory loops and submit batches without MCP when you only need the CLI install → run → submit path and do not need Cursor to call Factory Session tools. Prefer another MCP host guide when your editor or agent is not Cursor, even if it also speaks MCP.

stepsOrWorkflow.title: Steps Or Workflow
stepsOrWorkflow.body: Confirm the you CLI is installed, add a Cursor Model Context Protocol (MCP) server entry for you mcp serve, reload Cursor so the child process starts, then use Factory Session tools for a first validate and start or status loop.

commonPitfalls.title: Common Pitfalls
commonPitfalls.body: Common setup mistakes include a missing you binary on PATH, the wrong project cwd, and forgetting to reload Cursor after MCP config changes.

mcpConfigLocations.body: Put the server entry in the project file at .cursor/mcp.json when the workflow lives in that repository, or in the global file at ~/.cursor/mcp.json when you want the same server across projects. Project config is usually enough for one workflow root.

mcpReload.body: Replace command with the absolute path to your you binary when Cursor cannot see your shell PATH. Keep args as mcp serve. Set cwd to the workflow project root so sources resolve. After you save the file, restart Cursor or run Developer: Reload Window so Cursor respawns the MCP child process.
